name: TDD-STATE-MACHINE-BUG-4
title: tdd-step.py tdd_state transition in feature.json left unstaged after TDD cycle completes
description:
After the TDD subagent completes the UNLOCK step, feature.json reflects the final tdd_state (e.g. test-green) but the change is left unstaged — not committed to the branch. The dispatcher must manually commit it before the PR can be merged cleanly.

Observed: after merging PR #96 (rabbit-cage permissions), git status showed .claude/features/rabbit-cage/feature.json as modified with tdd_state: test-red → test-green. The TDD subagent had advanced the state but tdd-step.py (or the subagent) did not commit the file.

Root cause: tdd-step.py transitions the tdd_state field in feature.json but does not stage or commit the file. The subagent is expected to commit it as part of the UNLOCK step, but either the instruction is missing or not followed.

Fix: the UNLOCK step in the TDD subagent prompt (dispatch-tdd-subagent.py) should explicitly git add feature.json && git commit after tdd-step.py advances to test-green. Alternatively, tdd-step.py itself should stage and commit feature.json as part of the transition. The commit message should follow the pattern: chore(<feature>): advance tdd_state to test-green.

Related: TDD-STATE-MACHINE-BUG-3 (spec.md also not committed — same structural gap, different file).

history.note: Fixed by PR #99: dispatch-tdd-subagent.py UNLOCK step now commits feature.json before HANDOFF. Inv 17 + test coverage.
